title: The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history

processed.summary:
- The **IBM 4 Pi** series adapted the **System/360 architecture** for aerospace, providing a standardized instruction set and software compatibility for military and space applications.
- These systems utilized **radiation-hardened components** and specialized read-only storage (ROS) to ensure high reliability in extreme environments like low-earth orbit.
- The architecture evolved from 16-bit to 32-bit processing, successfully bridging the gap between **terrestrial mainframes** and miniaturized, ruggedized flight hardware.
- Its deployment in the **Space Shuttle** and B-52 bomber established the standard for fault-tolerant, modular computing in critical avionics systems.
key_results: Successful miniaturization of S/360 mainframe logic for aerospace use.
Provided the primary flight control for the Space Shuttle via the AP-101 model.
Integrated magnetic core memory transitioning to semiconductor memory over its lifecycle.
Achieved significant weight and power reductions compared to contemporary vacuum-tube systems.
Standardized military avionics under a unified software development ecosystem.
processed.one_sentence_takeaway: Ken Shirriff chronicles how IBM adapted mainframe architectures into the 4 Pi series to power historic aerospace missions.

title: LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs

processed.summary:
- Community analysis on Hacker News highlights **significant memory consumption** in LinkedIn's web application, which was observed utilizing 2.4 GB of RAM for only two browser tabs.
- The discussion serves as a case study in **browser resource bloat**, illustrating how complex tracking scripts and modern SPA frameworks impact overall system performance.
- The thread underscores a growing conflict in **resource allocation**, where bloated web architectures increasingly compete with local compute tasks such as **Local LLMs** and developer environments.
key_results: LinkedIn was documented consuming 2.4 GB of RAM across two active browser tabs.
The post received over 600 points, indicating a high level of industry concern regarding web performance.
Highlights the overhead inherent in modern JavaScript-heavy frontend application architectures.
User commentary suggests that single-page applications (SPAs) are increasingly failing to manage memory lifecycle effectively.
Reflects a broader trend of declining resource efficiency in mainstream SaaS platforms.
processed.one_sentence_takeaway: Hacker News reports that LinkedIn's web application consumes 2.4 GB of RAM, highlighting significant resource bloat in modern frontend architectures.

title: Canadian man says U.S. border officers made him give DNA sample

processed.summary:
- Reports on **U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP)** enforcing mandatory biometric data acquisition protocols for non-U.S. citizens detained at border crossings.
- Details the workflow where **DNA samples** are collected via cheek swabs and submitted to the FBI’s **CODIS database** for permanent storage and profiling.
- Highlights the expanding scope of the **DNA Fingerprint Act of 2005**, which has moved from criminal suspects to general detainee populations.
- Discusses the **legal and privacy impact** of centralized genetic data collection on international relations and individual civil liberties.
key_results: U.S. CBP mandated DNA collection from a Canadian citizen despite no criminal charges.
Data collection is authorized under the expanded DNA Fingerprint Act of 2005.
Samples are processed by the FBI and integrated into the national CODIS database.
Privacy advocates express concern over the lack of data deletion protocols for non-citizens.
Legislative pushback is emerging from U.S. representatives regarding biometric surveillance at borders.
processed.one_sentence_takeaway: CBC News reports that U.S. Customs and Border Protection is enforcing mandatory DNA collection for detained non-U.S. citizens.

title: Show HN: Twitch Roulette – Find live streamers who need views the most
summary:
Hey HN, I re-launched an old site I remembered back in the day that someone made called twitchroulette.net with a lot of new features and stats and I would love for people to check it out. The idea is you can easily browse the less browsed parts of twitch and find cool and new streamers to say hi to, and maybe make some new friends.

I also added some real time stats and breakdowns per channel and I think some of the things they show are pretty interesting. Check it out!

Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47549160

Points: 176

# Comments: 94

processed.summary:
• Re-launch of **Twitch Roulette**, a web-based discovery platform designed to surface low-viewership streamers via randomized search filters.
• Implementation of **real-time data analytics** and statistical breakdowns for individual channels to provide contextual viewer data.
• Focus on surfacing the **long-tail distribution** of content creators to facilitate community discovery in the less-browsed segments of the platform.
key_results: Re-launched the twitchroulette.net platform with modernized features.
